== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}

{{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}}
is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] descended from {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their shrine is {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="www.norichan.jp">{{Cite web|title=夜疑神社|url=http://www.norichan.jp/jinja/benkyou2/yagi.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=www.norichan.jp}}</ref> Where they worshipped their ancestral deity {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2016-08-18|title=��^�_�Ђ̌�Ր_|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160818202730/http://orange.zero.jp/hara.park/saijin.html|access-date=2024-03-23|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="www.norichan.jp" />

They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}}
is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] descended from {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}}
is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] descended from {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their shrine is {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="www.norichan.jp">{{Cite web|title=夜疑神社|url=http://www.norichan.jp/jinja/benkyou2/yagi.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=www.norichan.jp}}</ref> Where they worshipped their ancestral deity {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2016-08-18|title=��^�_�Ђ̌�Ր_|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160818202730/http://orange.zero.jp/hara.park/saijin.html|access-date=2024-03-23|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="www.norichan.jp" />

They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Expand Japanese}}

{{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}}
is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] descended from {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their shrine is {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="www.norichan.jp">{{Cite web|title=夜疑神社|url=http://www.norichan.jp/jinja/benkyou2/yagi.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=www.norichan.jp}}</ref> Where they worshipped their ancestral deity {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2016-08-18|title=��^�_�Ђ̌�Ր_|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160818202730/http://orange.zero.jp/hara.park/saijin.html|access-date=2024-03-23|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="www.norichan.jp" />

They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Expand Japanese}}

{{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}}
is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] descended from {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their shrine is {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="www.norichan.jp">{{Cite web|title=夜疑神社|url=http://www.norichan.jp/jinja/benkyou2/yagi.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=www.norichan.jp}}</ref> Where they worshipped their ancestral deity {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2016-08-18|title=��^�_�Ђ̌�Ր_|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160818202730/http://orange.zero.jp/hara.park/saijin.html|access-date=2024-03-23|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="www.norichan.jp" />

They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}}
is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] descended from {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their shrine is {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="www.norichan.jp">{{Cite web|title=夜疑神社|url=http://www.norichan.jp/jinja/benkyou2/yagi.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=www.norichan.jp}}</ref> Where they worshipped their ancestral deity {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2016-08-18|title=��^�_�Ђ̌�Ր_|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160818202730/http://orange.zero.jp/hara.park/saijin.html|access-date=2024-03-23|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="www.norichan.jp" />

They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}}
is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] descended from {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their shrine is {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="www.norichan.jp">{{Cite web|title=夜疑神社|url=http://www.norichan.jp/jinja/benkyou2/yagi.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=www.norichan.jp}}</ref> Where they worshipped their ancestral deity {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2016-08-18|title=��^�_�Ђ̌�Ր_|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160818202730/http://orange.zero.jp/hara.park/saijin.html|access-date=2024-03-23|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="www.norichan.jp" />

They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Expand Japanese}}

{{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}}
is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] descended from {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their shrine is {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="www.norichan.jp">{{Cite web|title=夜疑神社|url=http://www.norichan.jp/jinja/benkyou2/yagi.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=www.norichan.jp}}</ref> Where they worshipped their ancestral deity {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2016-08-18|title=��^�_�Ђ̌�Ր_|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160818202730/http://orange.zero.jp/hara.park/saijin.html|access-date=2024-03-23|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="www.norichan.jp" />

They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}}
is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] descended from {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their shrine is {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="www.norichan.jp">{{Cite web|title=夜疑神社|url=http://www.norichan.jp/jinja/benkyou2/yagi.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=www.norichan.jp}}</ref> Where they worshipped their ancestral deity {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2016-08-18|title=��^�_�Ђ̌�Ր_|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160818202730/http://orange.zero.jp/hara.park/saijin.html|access-date=2024-03-23|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="www.norichan.jp" />

They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}

{{Expand Japanese}}

{{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}}
is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] descended from {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their shrine is {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="www.norichan.jp">{{Cite web|title=夜疑神社|url=http://www.norichan.jp/jinja/benkyou2/yagi.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=www.norichan.jp}}</ref> Where they worshipped their ancestral deity {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2016-08-18|title=��^�_�Ђ̌�Ր_|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160818202730/http://orange.zero.jp/hara.park/saijin.html|access-date=2024-03-23|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="www.norichan.jp" />

They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}

{{Expand Japanese}}

{{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}}
is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] descended from {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their shrine is {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="www.norichan.jp">{{Cite web|title=夜疑神社|url=http://www.norichan.jp/jinja/benkyou2/yagi.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=www.norichan.jp}}</ref> Where they worshipped their ancestral deity {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2016-08-18|title=��^�_�Ђ̌�Ր_|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160818202730/http://orange.zero.jp/hara.park/saijin.html|access-date=2024-03-23|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="www.norichan.jp" />

They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Expand Japanese}}

{{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}}
is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] descended from {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their shrine is {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="www.norichan.jp">{{Cite web|title=夜疑神社|url=http://www.norichan.jp/jinja/benkyou2/yagi.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=www.norichan.jp}}</ref> Where they worshipped their ancestral deity {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2016-08-18|title=��^�_�Ђ̌�Ր_|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160818202730/http://orange.zero.jp/hara.park/saijin.html|access-date=2024-03-23|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="www.norichan.jp" />

They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}}
is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] descended from {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their shrine is {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="www.norichan.jp">{{Cite web|title=夜疑神社|url=http://www.norichan.jp/jinja/benkyou2/yagi.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=www.norichan.jp}}</ref> Where they worshipped their ancestral deity {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2016-08-18|title=��^�_�Ђ̌�Ր_|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160818202730/http://orange.zero.jp/hara.park/saijin.html|access-date=2024-03-23|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="www.norichan.jp" />

They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Expand Japanese}}

{{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}}
is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] descended from {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their shrine is {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="www.norichan.jp">{{Cite web|title=夜疑神社|url=http://www.norichan.jp/jinja/benkyou2/yagi.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=www.norichan.jp}}</ref> Where they worshipped their ancestral deity {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2016-08-18|title=��^�_�Ђ̌�Ր_|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160818202730/http://orange.zero.jp/hara.park/saijin.html|access-date=2024-03-23|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="www.norichan.jp" />

They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}

{{Expand Japanese}}

{{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}}
is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] descended from {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their shrine is {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="www.norichan.jp">{{Cite web|title=夜疑神社|url=http://www.norichan.jp/jinja/benkyou2/yagi.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=www.norichan.jp}}</ref> Where they worshipped their ancestral deity {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2016-08-18|title=��^�_�Ђ̌�Ր_|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160818202730/http://orange.zero.jp/hara.park/saijin.html|access-date=2024-03-23|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="www.norichan.jp" />

They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}

== History ==
It is unknown when the {{ill|Yagi clan|ja|八木氏|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|8=en:draft|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi clan|qq=}} built {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}, but it is also mentioned in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki]], which was created during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=御由緒 {{!}} 夜疑神社【大阪府岸和田市】交通安全・厄除け祈願|url=https://www.yagi-jinja.jp/about/|access-date=2024-03-23|website=www.yagi-jinja.jp}}</ref>. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} compiled in the early Heian period, there is a description indicates that the Yagi clan, an ancient powerful clan, enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Furutama|8=en:draft|9=Furutama-no-Mikoto|10=fr|11=Furutama-no-Mikoto|lt=Furutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.  {{Sfn|岸和田市観光振興協会|1993}} . 

{{Short description|Japanese Kamakura-period castle}}

The Yagi clan ruled {{ill|Yagi Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Castle|qq=}} and the surrounding area for centuries. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, Tajima came under the control of the powerful {{ill|Yamana clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana clan|qq=}}, and the Yagi accepted the Yamana as overlords. However, following the {{ill|Onin War|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onin War|qq=}} the power of the Yamana was greatly weakened. In 1575, the 15th castellan of Yagi Castle, Yagi Toyonobu was approached by both the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}} from the west, and {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} from the east. Initially, the Yagi cooperated with the Mōri, but in 1579 surrendered to Nobunaga's general, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hidenaga|qq=}} in exchange for being allowed to keep their castle and territory. As per the terms of his surrender, Yagi Toyonobu was ordered to lead an army against the Mōri at {{ill|Tottori Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Castle|qq=}} in 1580 and was ordered togaed Wakasa Oni-ga-jo Castle (also in Tottori). He was defeated in a counterattack by Mori forces in 1581, and went missing-in-action. Consequently, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}  seized Yagi Castle and awarded it to {{ill|Bessho Shigemune|ja|別所重宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Shigemune|1=Bessho Shigemune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396808}} as part of a 15,000 ''{{ill|koku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koku|qq=}}'' fief.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=draft}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

They owned {{ill|Wakasa Oniga Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Oniga Castle|qq=}}
